steps.title: Find what the passage proves about three-deck ships
steps.content: The passage tells you that both the Dreadnought and the Royal George have three decks, yet only the Royal George is ranked first-rate. That single fact is the logical foundation for the conclusion the blank must express.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice and confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• Choice A — the Dreadnought had between 850 and 875 crewmen.: 850–875 is the crew range for first-rate ships; the Dreadnought is not first-rate, so it cannot have a first-rate crew. ✗
  • Choice B — all ships with at least 98 cannons were ranked as first-rate ships.: The Dreadnought has 98 cannons and is explicitly not first-rate, directly contradicting this claim. ✗
  • Choice C — the Royal George needed a crew larger than 875 people in order to operate efficiently.: The passage says nothing about operational needs; this introduces information not supported by the text. ✗
  • Choice D — not all ships with three decks were ranked as first-rate ships.: Both ships have three decks but only one is first-rate—this conclusion follows directly from the evidence. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
